latest.title: Royalty Deed and Back-in Rights for the Kenya SPA
latest.content:
Today 15:42
RNS Number : 1474M
Tullow Oil PLC
13 July 2026
Tullow Oil plc
Tullow agrees to terminate the Royalty Deed and Back-in Rights in the Kenya Shares Purchase Agreement for $9 million additional consideration
13 July 2026
-
Tullow Oil plc (Tullow)
is pleased to
announce that its wholly owned subsidiary, Tullow Overseas Holdings BV (TOHBV), has agreed to increase the consideration for the shares in Tullow Kenya B.V. sold to Auron Energy E&P Limited by a further $9 million. The Company has also agreed to terminate its rights to Kenyan royalty payments and back-in right with Gulf Energy Limited (Transaction).
Ian Perks, Chief Executive Officer of Tullow, commented:
"This transaction is another important step in our strategy to deliver value from our portfolio and strengthen the balance sheet. By accelerating the receipt of $9 million from the sale of the shares in Tullow Kenya B.V., we are securing near-term cash proceeds and simplifying our portfolio. It also demonstrates the continued momentum across the business as we deliver against our strategic priorities and maintain a disciplined approach to capital allocation."
Transaction structure
·
The Transaction is further to the Sale and Purchase Agreement (SPA) signed between TOHBV and Auron Energy E&P Limited, an affiliate of Gulf Energy Ltd, in July 2025 for the sale and purchase of 100% of the shares in Tullow Kenya BV, which consisted of:
§
Tranche A:
$40 million payable on transaction completion (received 25 September 2025).
§
Tranche B:
$40 million payable at the earlier of FDP approval or 30 June 2026 (received 9 March 2026).
§
Tranche C:
$40 million payable no later than 30 June 2033, which remains payable to Tullow, subject to the following payment schedule:
-
Payments of $2 million per quarter starting in the third quarter of 2028, provided Dated Brent oil price averaged at least $65/bbl during the preceding quarter.
-
If $40 million in aggregate has not been paid by 30 June 2033, the remainder will be due as a bullet payment at that point irrespective of the prevailing oil price.
§
Royalty payment
: In addition
to the above, TOHBV will receive quarterly royalty payments of $0.5/bbl multiplied by 80% of total production, subject to oil price, resource and production related conditions to the extent that the Purchaser retains its interest in the Disposed Assets.
§
Back-in right:
option for 30% participation in potential future development phases.
·
The Transaction announced today represents the termination of royalty payments and back-in rights; Tranche C is not affected by the Transaction and remains payable.
·
Proceeds from the Transaction will be used to strengthen Tullow's balance sheet.
·
Completion of the transaction and receipt of funds is expected not later than 17 July 2026.
CONTACTS
Tullow Investor Relations
ir@tullowoil.com
Matthew Evans
Camarco (Media)
(+44 20 3757 4980)

Georgia Edmonds
Rebecca Waterworth

latest.title: Repayment of c.$48 million of Senior Notes
latest.content:
Today 09:32
RNS Number : 5622J
Tullow Oil PLC
24 June 2026
Tullow Oil plc
Repayment of c.$48 million of Senior Notes
24 June 2026
-
Tullow Oil plc (Tullow)
announces a c.$48 million repayment of its outstanding Senior Secured Notes due 2028 (the "Notes").
Richard Miller, Chief Financial Officer of Tullow, commented:
"This partial repayment of the Notes has been enabled by our strong operational and financial performance in 2026. Being able to take this meaningful step only two months after closing the refinancing transaction is reflective of our disciplined approach to capital allocation and the quality of our assets. We continue to focus on deleveraging and exploring longer term refinancing and strategic options, underpinned by our enhanced value proposition."
Key redemption details
·
Issuer:
Tullow Holdco 2 Limited
·
Notes:
10.25% Senior Secured Notes due 2028
·
ISIN:
USG9131AAA19 (Reg S) and US89941AAA07 (Rule 144A)
CUSIP:
G9131A AA1 (Reg S) and 89941A AA0 (Rule 144A)
·
Redemption date
:
24 June 2026
·
Principal amount repaid (Redeemed Notes):
$48,211,589.95
·
Redemption Price:
101% of the principal amount
·
Interest
: the following cash payments in respect of interest accrued between 24 April 2026 (the "Closing Date") and the Redemption Date have been made on the Redemption Date:
o
uncapitalised Mandatory PIK Interest on the Redeemed Notes;
o
Mandatory Cash Interest on the Redeemed Notes;
o
uncapitalised PIYC Interest on the outstanding principal amount of the Notes prior to the Redemption Date.
·
Remaining outstanding principal post repayment:
$1,162,034,475
Capitalized terms used but not defined herein have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Indenture.
Execution mechanism
The redemption is executed in accordance with the provisions outlined in the indenture dated as of 17 May 2021 and as amended and restated on 10 April 2026 (as amended, restated, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time, the "Indenture").
In accordance with the Indenture the redemption will take place on the basis of a pro rata pass-through distribution of principal. No Notes of $1,000 or less will be redeemed in part.
Partial redemption including reduction of Global Notes and issuance of replacement Definitive Registered Notes if applicable will be effected in accordance with DTC procedures.

company_30s.what_they_do: Tullow Oil is a mid‑size oil and gas exploration company that operates mainly in Africa, developing new fields and producing crude for global markets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company taps into large untapped oil reserves in regions where supply is still limited. Its projects help diversify the global energy mix and provide a foothold in high‑growth emerging markets.
company_30s.current_state: Tullow is in a restructuring phase: it has just paid back a big chunk of senior debt, refreshed its capital structure, and is working on new wells. The stock is trading very low, reflecting the market’s uncertainty about the company’s near‑term cash flow.

rns_narrative: The company is focused on debt cleanup – a $48 m note was repaid and a refinancing deal closed – which clears a key obstacle for future investment.
Governance moves – new directors and a revised voting structure – signal a fresh start for board oversight.
Operational updates – a royalty deed and back‑in rights in Kenya – show the company is keeping its core projects alive while looking for new production.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but hopeful – people are mostly silent, with a few bullish chatter about oil prices and cargo deals.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to rising oil prices and the company’s new cargo shipments as a sign the stock could rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that debt still weighs heavily and that production may lag, keeping the price stuck near zero.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone is steady but slightly improving; a few bullish posts have appeared after the latest AGM, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent search interest, which means a surprise catalyst could hit hard.

near_term_catalysts: Oil prices climbing above $80 a barrel – that’s the level where the company’s cargo contracts start to generate meaningful cash flow.
A clear announcement that the new wells in Kenya will start production in Q3 – that would validate the company’s operational plans.
A spike in trading volume that breaks the 50‑day moving average – a sign that traders are beginning to take notice.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in oil‑related volume, a press release about new production, or a sharp rise in the company’s own share price after a news event.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the very early phase of a potential recovery. If capitulation is complete, the next few weeks could see a quick bounce once oil prices or production news arrive.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the setup is too uncertain for a confident bullish stance yet.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If oil prices stay above $80 and the new wells deliver, the share could climb from near zero to the mid‑single‑digit pence range – a 200‑300 % upside. The deep crash offers a high‑reward, low‑probability play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Debt remains a risk; if oil prices fall or production delays occur, the share could stay flat or fall further. A worst‑case scenario is a continued slide to a sub‑penny level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: almost 100 % of the current price (the share is already near zero). Potential gain: 200‑300 % if the price rebounds to the 50‑pence mark. The asymmetry is favorable for a contrarian who can stomach a total wipe.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 30‑pence support, allocate a small portion of your portfolio, and set a stop just below the 20‑pence level. Size the position so that a 10‑pence drop wipes out no more than 5 % of your capital.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until oil prices cross $80 or until a new production announcement is made. Watch the 50‑day volume average – a spike above 1.5× that average is a strong buying signal.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a board‑level bankruptcy filing or a delisting notice, exit immediately. No other terminal risks are evident at present.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TLW.L sits in a deep slump with no clear catalyst yet; a spike in oil prices or a new production announcement could trigger a big upside, but the risk of staying flat remains high.
